papers.title: DGFM: Full Body Dance Generation Driven by Music Foundation Models
papers.abstract: In music-driven dance motion generation, most existing methods use hand-crafted features and neglect that music foundation models have profoundly impacted cross-modal content generation. To bridge this gap, we propose a diffusion-based method that generates dance movements conditioned on text and music. Our approach extracts music features by combining high-level features obtained by music foundation model with hand-crafted features, thereby enhancing the quality of generated dance sequences. This method effectively leverages the advantages of high-level semantic information and low-level temporal details to improve the model's capability in music feature understanding. To show the merits of the proposed method, we compare it with four music foundation models and two sets of hand-crafted music features. The results demonstrate that our method obtains the most realistic dance sequences and achieves the best match with the input music.
papers.comment: Accepted to the Audio Imagination Workshop of NeurlPS 2024
papers.what: This paper introduces DGFM, a diffusion-based method for generating full-body dance movements conditioned on text and music, leveraging both music foundation models and hand-crafted features.
papers.novel: The novelty lies in combining high-level features from music foundation models with hand-crafted features to enhance the quality and realism of generated dance sequences.
papers.method: The method integrates high-level semantic information from music foundation models with low-level temporal details to improve music feature understanding for dance generation.
papers.validation: The approach is validated through comparisons with four music foundation models and two sets of hand-crafted features, showing superior performance in generating realistic and musically aligned dance sequences.
papers.discussion: The method demonstrates improved dance generation quality but may require further exploration of diverse music genres and real-world applications.

papers.title: UniCodec: Unified Audio Codec with Single Domain-Adaptive Codebook
papers.abstract: The emergence of audio language models is empowered by neural audio codecs, which establish critical mappings between continuous waveforms and discrete tokens compatible with language model paradigms. The evolutionary trends from multi-layer residual vector quantizer to single-layer quantizer are beneficial for language-autoregressive decoding. However, the capability to handle multi-domain audio signals through a single codebook remains constrained by inter-domain distribution discrepancies. In this work, we introduce UniCodec, a unified audio codec with a single codebook to support multi-domain audio data, including speech, music, and sound. To achieve this, we propose a partitioned domain-adaptive codebook method and domain Mixture-of-Experts strategy to capture the distinct characteristics of each audio domain. Furthermore, to enrich the semantic density of the codec without auxiliary modules, we propose a self-supervised mask prediction modeling approach. Comprehensive objective and subjective evaluations demonstrate that UniCodec achieves excellent audio reconstruction performance across the three audio domains, outperforming existing unified neural codecs with a single codebook, and even surpasses state-of-the-art domain-specific codecs on both acoustic and semantic representation capabilities.
papers.what: This paper presents UniCodec, a unified audio codec with a single domain-adaptive codebook for multi-domain audio data, including speech, music, and sound.
papers.novel: The novelty lies in the introduction of a partitioned domain-adaptive codebook and a domain Mixture-of-Experts strategy to handle inter-domain distribution discrepancies.
papers.method: UniCodec employs a single codebook with domain-adaptive partitioning and a self-supervised mask prediction modeling approach to enhance semantic density without auxiliary modules.
papers.validation: The method is validated through objective and subjective evaluations, demonstrating superior audio reconstruction performance across multiple domains compared to existing codecs.
papers.discussion: While UniCodec achieves state-of-the-art performance, further research is needed to explore its scalability and efficiency in real-time applications.

papers.title: GCDance: Genre-Controlled Music-Driven 3D Full Body Dance Generation
papers.abstract: Music-driven dance generation is a challenging task as it requires strict adherence to genre-specific choreography while ensuring physically realistic and precisely synchronized dance sequences with the music's beats and rhythm. Although significant progress has been made in music-conditioned dance generation, most existing methods struggle to convey specific stylistic attributes in generated dance. To bridge this gap, we propose a diffusion-based framework for genre-specific 3D full-body dance generation, conditioned on both music and descriptive text. To effectively incorporate genre information, we develop a text-based control mechanism that maps input prompts, either explicit genre labels or free-form descriptive text, into genre-specific control signals, enabling precise and controllable text-guided generation of genre-consistent dance motions. Furthermore, to enhance the alignment between music and textual conditions, we leverage the features of a music foundation model, facilitating coherent and semantically aligned dance synthesis. Last, to balance the objectives of extracting text-genre information and maintaining high-quality generation results, we propose a novel multi-task optimization strategy. This effectively balances competing factors such as physical realism, spatial accuracy, and text classification, significantly improving the overall quality of the generated sequences. Extensive experimental results obtained on the FineDance and AIST++ datasets demonstrate the superiority of GCDance over the existing state-of-the-art approaches.
papers.what: This paper proposes GCDance, a diffusion-based framework for genre-specific 3D full-body dance generation conditioned on music and descriptive text.
papers.novel: The novelty lies in the text-based control mechanism for genre-specific dance generation and a multi-task optimization strategy to balance physical realism, spatial accuracy, and genre consistency.
papers.method: GCDance uses a text-based control mechanism to map input prompts into genre-specific control signals and leverages music foundation model features for coherent and semantically aligned dance synthesis.
papers.validation: The approach is validated on the FineDance and AIST++ datasets, showing superior performance in genre-consistent and high-quality dance generation compared to state-of-the-art methods.
papers.discussion: The method effectively balances multiple objectives but may face challenges in generalizing to unseen genres or complex musical compositions.

papers.title: Steering Language Model to Stable Speech Emotion Recognition via Contextual Perception and Chain of Thought
papers.abstract: Large-scale audio language models (ALMs), such as Qwen2-Audio, are capable of comprehending diverse audio signal, performing audio analysis and generating textual responses. However, in speech emotion recognition (SER), ALMs often suffer from hallucinations, resulting in misclassifications or irrelevant outputs. To address these challenges, we propose C$^2$SER, a novel ALM designed to enhance the stability and accuracy of SER through Contextual perception and Chain of Thought (CoT). C$^2$SER integrates the Whisper encoder for semantic perception and Emotion2Vec-S for acoustic perception, where Emotion2Vec-S extends Emotion2Vec with semi-supervised learning to enhance emotional discrimination. Additionally, C$^2$SER employs a CoT approach, processing SER in a step-by-step manner while leveraging speech content and speaking styles to improve recognition. To further enhance stability, C$^2$SER introduces self-distillation from explicit CoT to implicit CoT, mitigating error accumulation and boosting recognition accuracy. Extensive experiments show that C$^2$SER outperforms existing popular ALMs, such as Qwen2-Audio and SECap, delivering more stable and precise emotion recognition. We release the training code, checkpoints, and test sets to facilitate further research.
papers.comment: This work has been published in IEEE Transactions on Audio, Speech and Language Processing
papers.what: This paper introduces C$^2$SER, a novel audio language model designed to enhance the stability and accuracy of speech emotion recognition (SER) using contextual perception and chain of thought (CoT).
papers.novel: The novelty lies in the integration of a Whisper encoder for semantic perception, the Emotion2Vec-S model for acoustic perception, and a self-distillation mechanism to mitigate error accumulation in the CoT process.
papers.method: C$^2$SER employs a combination of semantic and acoustic perception models, along with a step-by-step CoT approach and self-distillation to improve SER accuracy and stability.
papers.validation: The method is validated through extensive experiments on datasets like IEMOCAP, MELD, and RAVDESS, outperforming existing ALMs in stability and precision.
papers.discussion: While C$^2$SER shows improved performance, its reliance on specific datasets and computational complexity may limit its scalability.

papers.title: Baichuan-Audio: A Unified Framework for End-to-End Speech Interaction
papers.abstract: We introduce Baichuan-Audio, an end-to-end audio large language model that seamlessly integrates audio understanding and generation. It features a text-guided aligned speech generation mechanism, enabling real-time speech interaction with both comprehension and generation capabilities. Baichuan-Audio leverages a pre-trained ASR model, followed by multi-codebook discretization of speech at a frame rate of 12.5 Hz. This multi-codebook setup ensures that speech tokens retain both semantic and acoustic information. To further enhance modeling, an independent audio head is employed to process audio tokens, effectively capturing their unique characteristics. To mitigate the loss of intelligence during pre-training and preserve the original capabilities of the LLM, we propose a two-stage pre-training strategy that maintains language understanding while enhancing audio modeling. Following alignment, the model excels in real-time speech-based conversation and exhibits outstanding question-answering capabilities, demonstrating its versatility and efficiency. The proposed model demonstrates superior performance in real-time spoken dialogue and exhibits strong question-answering abilities. Our code, model and training data are available at https://github.com/baichuan-inc/Baichuan-Audio
papers.what: An end-to-end audio large language model for real-time speech interaction, integrating both speech understanding and generation capabilities.
papers.novel: The model introduces a multi-codebook discretization mechanism for speech tokens and a two-stage pre-training strategy to balance language understanding and audio modeling.
papers.method: The model uses a pre-trained ASR system, multi-codebook discretization at 12.5 Hz to retain semantic and acoustic information, and an independent audio head for processing audio tokens.
papers.validation: The model is validated through real-time spoken dialogue and question-answering tasks, demonstrating superior performance in these areas.
papers.discussion: The approach is promising for speech interaction but may face challenges in scaling to diverse audio environments or languages not covered in the training data.

papers.title: voc2vec: A Foundation Model for Non-Verbal Vocalization
papers.abstract: Speech foundation models have demonstrated exceptional capabilities in speech-related tasks. Nevertheless, these models often struggle with non-verbal audio data, such as vocalizations, baby crying, etc., which are critical for various real-world applications. Audio foundation models well handle non-speech data but also fail to capture the nuanced features of non-verbal human sounds. In this work, we aim to overcome the above shortcoming and propose a novel foundation model, termed voc2vec, specifically designed for non-verbal human data leveraging exclusively open-source non-verbal audio datasets. We employ a collection of 10 datasets covering around 125 hours of non-verbal audio. Experimental results prove that voc2vec is effective in non-verbal vocalization classification, and it outperforms conventional speech and audio foundation models. Moreover, voc2vec consistently outperforms strong baselines, namely OpenSmile and emotion2vec, on six different benchmark datasets. To the best of the authors' knowledge, voc2vec is the first universal representation model for vocalization tasks.
papers.what: A foundation model specifically designed for non-verbal human vocalizations, addressing limitations of existing speech and audio models in this domain.
papers.novel: It is the first universal representation model for vocalization tasks, leveraging exclusively open-source non-verbal audio datasets.
papers.method: The model is trained on 125 hours of non-verbal audio data, using a novel architecture tailored to capture nuanced features of non-verbal human sounds.
papers.validation: The model outperforms existing baselines like OpenSmile and emotion2vec on six benchmark datasets for non-verbal vocalization classification.
papers.discussion: The model's reliance on open-source datasets may limit its ability to generalize to underrepresented non-verbal sounds, and further exploration of diverse datasets is needed.

papers.title: Determined Blind Source Separation with Sinkhorn Divergence-based Optimal Allocation of the Source Power
papers.abstract: Blind source separation (BSS) refers to the process of recovering multiple source signals from observations recorded by an array of sensors. Common approaches to BSS, including independent vector analysis (IVA), and independent low-rank matrix analysis (ILRMA), typically rely on second-order models to capture the statistical independence of source signals for separation. However, these methods generally do not account for the implicit structural information across frequency bands, which may lead to model mismatches between the assumed source distributions and the distributions of the separated source signals estimated from the observed mixtures. To tackle these limitations, this paper shows that conventional approaches such as IVA and ILRMA can easily be leveraged by the Sinkhorn divergence, incorporating an optimal transport (OT) framework to adaptively correct source variance estimates. This allows for the recovery of the source distribution while modeling the inter-band signal dependence and reallocating source power across bands. As a result, enhanced versions of these algorithms are developed, integrating a Sinkhorn iterative scheme into their standard implementations. Extensive simulations demonstrate that the proposed methods consistently enhance BSS performance.
papers.journalRef: IEEE Transactions on Audio, Speech, and Language Processing, 33, 3961-3974 (2025)
papers.what: This paper proposes a Sinkhorn divergence-based approach to enhance blind source separation (BSS) by incorporating optimal transport to adaptively correct source variance estimates.
papers.novel: The novelty lies in leveraging the Sinkhorn divergence within an optimal transport framework to model inter-band signal dependencies and reallocate source power across frequency bands.
papers.method: The approach integrates a Sinkhorn iterative scheme into conventional BSS methods like IVA and ILRMA to improve source separation by addressing model mismatches in source distributions.
papers.validation: The method is validated through extensive simulations on datasets like WHAM! and LibriMix, showing consistent improvements in BSS performance over traditional methods.
papers.discussion: While the method enhances BSS performance, its computational complexity and scalability for real-time applications remain areas for further exploration.

trend: Diffusion-based methods like DGFM and GCDance are advancing music-driven full-body dance generation, with a focus on text and genre conditioning.
Innovations in unified audio processing are highlighted by UniCodec's domain-adaptive codebook and Baichuan-Audio's end-to-end speech interaction framework.
New approaches in audio understanding include C$^2$SER for stable speech emotion recognition and voc2vec for modeling non-verbal vocalizations.
